title: 👤 Per-User Copilot Metrics [Edu]
description: Shows per-user Copilot activity metrics — interactions, code volume, acceptance rate, feature adoption, and daily trends — filtered to a selected user via the variable picker.

list.name: user_login
list.label: User
list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT user_login FROM copilot_user_daily ORDER BY user_login
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT user_login FROM copilot_user_daily ORDER BY user_login

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE('Live: ' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'org') || '/' || (SELECT value FROM app_config WHERE key = 'repo'), 'Not yet synced') AS "Data Source"

options.content:
# 👤 Per-User Copilot Metrics

**Supplementary Metrics — Individual Developer Copilot Activity**

Use the **User** dropdown above to select a developer and explore their Copilot activity across the selected time range.

## What it Measures
This dashboard surfaces per-user data from the GitHub Copilot Metrics Reports API (users-28-day report). It shows how actively a specific developer is using Copilot — broken down by interaction volume, lines of code suggested and accepted, acceptance rate, and which Copilot surfaces they engage with.

⚠️ **Important:** This dashboard contains Copilot activity metrics only (interactions, code generation, lines accepted, and feature adoption). The GitHub Copilot Metrics Reports API does not provide per-user pull request telemetry, so this dashboard does not include PR-related metrics or per-PR attribution.

## Why it Matters
Org-level dashboards show aggregate trends, but individual coaching conversations require user-level context. This dashboard helps engineering managers and developer coaches:
- Identify developers who have a seat but are not actively using Copilot
- Distinguish low-acceptance-rate users (may need prompting guidance) from high-volume users
- Understand feature adoption — does the developer use only completions, or also chat, CLI, agent mode, and code review?

## How to Interpret
| Metric | Healthy Signal | Action Needed |
|---|---|---|
| **Total Interactions** | Growing week-over-week | Flat or declining — explore blockers |
| **Acceptance Rate** | ≥30% | <20% — review prompting habits or suggestion quality |
| **Active Days** | Most days in window | Gaps — check for availability or disengagement |
| **Feature Adoption** | Multiple surfaces (✅ on several)  | Only completions — expand use-case awareness |

panels.description: Per-User Copilot Metrics — individual activity, code volume, acceptance rate, and feature adoption.

panels.title: Total Interactions (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(user_initiated_interaction_count) AS "Total Interactions (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: Copilot Metrics Reports API: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `user_initiated_interaction_count` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

Copilot Metrics Reports API (2026-03-10): `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `user_initiated_interaction_count`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Sum of `user_initiated_interaction_count` for the selected user across the Grafana time range. Each row represents one calendar day. An interaction is any user-driven Copilot event: accepting a suggestion, sending a chat message, running a CLI command, etc.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(user_initiated_interaction_count) AS "Total Interactions (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Code Generation Activities (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(code_generation_activity_count) AS "Code Generation Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `code_generation_activity_count` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `code_generation_activity_count`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Sum of `code_generation_activity_count` for the selected user. This counts Copilot code generation events — completions shown to the user, regardless of whether they were accepted.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(code_generation_activity_count) AS "Code Generation Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Code Acceptance Activities (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(code_acceptance_activity_count) AS "Code Acceptance Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `code_acceptance_activity_count` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `code_acceptance_activity_count`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Sum of `code_acceptance_activity_count` for the selected user. This counts the number of times the user explicitly accepted a Copilot code suggestion (Tab / ).

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(code_acceptance_activity_count) AS "Code Acceptance Activities (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Active Days (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT day) AS "Active Days (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Count of distinct calendar days where the user has a row in `copilot_user_daily`. The sync stores one row per user per day only when the user had Copilot activity. Days with no activity have no row.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT day) AS "Active Days (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Lines Suggested (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Sum of lines Copilot proposed to add for this user across all days in the time range. Includes both accepted and rejected suggestions.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Sum of lines the user accepted from Copilot suggestions. This is the numerator of the acceptance rate. A line is counted at the moment of acceptance, not when the code is committed.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0), 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

`SUM(loc_added_sum) / SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum)` expressed as a percentage. This is a line-weighted acceptance rate — a suggestion of 20 lines counts 20├ù more than a single-line suggestion.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0), 1
) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Feature Adoption (days used in window)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_agent THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Agent Mode (IDE)",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_chat THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Chat",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_cli THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "CLI",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_copilot_code_review_active THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Code Review (Active)",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_copilot_code_review_passive THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Code Review (Passive)",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_copilot_coding_agent THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Cloud Agent"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `used_agent`, `used_chat`, `used_cli`, `used_copilot_code_review_active`, `used_copilot_code_review_passive`, `used_copilot_coding_agent` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `used_agent`, `used_chat`, `used_cli`, `used_copilot_code_review_active`, `used_copilot_code_review_passive`, `used_copilot_coding_agent`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Each boolean column is converted into a day count using `SUM(CASE WHEN ... THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)` across all rows in the selected time range. The result is the number of days in the window where the selected user used each surface at least once.

### Feature Definitions

| Feature | Column | Set `true` when... |
|---|---|---|
| **Agent Mode (IDE)** | `used_agent` | Developer used Copilot agent mode inside an IDE |
| **Chat** | `used_chat` | Developer used Copilot Chat (IDE sidebar or web) |
| **CLI** | `used_cli` | Developer used GitHub Copilot CLI (`gh copilot`) |
| **Code Review (Active)** | `used_copilot_code_review_active` | Developer intentionally assigned Copilot as reviewer, re-requested a CCR review, or applied a CCR suggestion |
| **Code Review (Passive)** | `used_copilot_code_review_passive` | Copilot auto-reviewed a PR via repo policy; developer did not interact. Active takes precedence if both occur. |
| **Cloud Agent** | `used_copilot_coding_agent` | Developer assigned Copilot to a GitHub Issue or mentioned @copilot in a PR |

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_agent THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Agent Mode (IDE)",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_chat THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Chat",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_cli THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "CLI",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_copilot_code_review_active THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Code Review (Active)",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_copilot_code_review_passive THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Code Review (Passive)",
  SUM(CASE WHEN used_copilot_coding_agent THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS "Cloud Agent"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```

panels.title: Daily Activity Trend — Lines Accepted

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE(loc_added_sum, 0) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) AS "Lines Suggested"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE(loc_deleted_sum, 0) AS "Lines Deleted"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

panels.description: Source: `GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`, `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`, `loc_deleted_sum` | Tables: `copilot_user_daily`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `loc_added_sum`, `loc_suggested_to_add_sum`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Two time series plotted per calendar day:
- **Lines Accepted** (solid green) — `loc_added_sum` — lines accepted by the user that day
- **Lines Suggested** (dashed blue) — `loc_suggested_to_add_sum` — lines Copilot offered

The gap between the two lines is the volume of rejected suggestions. Shrinking gap = improving acceptance rate.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT day AS time, COALESCE(loc_added_sum, 0) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day
```

panels.title: Lines Deleted (28d)
panels.description: Lines deleted from the editor by Copilot (agent/edit mode direct file writes). Source: `loc_deleted_sum` | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_deleted_sum) AS "Lines Deleted (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.title: Net Lines Changed (28d)
panels.description: Net lines changed by Copilot (lines added minus lines deleted). Positive = net new code. Source: `loc_added_sum - loc_deleted_sum` | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) - COALESCE(SUM(loc_deleted_sum), 0) AS "Net Lines Changed (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.title: Top Languages Used with Copilot - Learning Guide
Top Languages Used with Copilot
panels.description: Languages used with Copilot, ranked by lines accepted. Acceptance Rate > 100% is expected when agent/CLI modes write more lines than were offered as completion suggestions — see the Learning Guide. Source: `totals_by_language_feature` JSONB | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  lang->>'language' AS "Language",
  SUM((lang->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint) AS "Lines Accepted",
  ROUND(
    SUM((lang->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint)::numeric
    / NULLIF(SUM((lang->>'loc_suggested_to_add_sum')::bigint), 0) * 100, 1
  ) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_language_feature) AS lang
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_language_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 10

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `totals_by_language_feature`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Unnests the `totals_by_language_feature` JSONB array with `jsonb_array_elements()`. Each element represents one language+feature combination. Groups by `language` (summing across features) to compute:

- **Lines Accepted** — `SUM(loc_added_sum)` — total Copilot-accepted lines in this language
- **Acceptance Rate (%)** — `SUM(loc_added_sum) / SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum)` per language

Sorted by Lines Accepted descending; capped at top 10 languages.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  lang->>'language' AS "Language",
  SUM((lang->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint) AS "Lines Accepted",
  ROUND(
    SUM((lang->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint)::numeric
    / NULLIF(SUM((lang->>'loc_suggested_to_add_sum')::bigint), 0) * 100, 1
  ) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_language_feature) AS lang
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_language_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 10
```

panels.title: Feature Activity Breakdown
panels.description: Copilot interactions and lines accepted by feature surface (completions, inline_chat, chat_panel, agent_edit). Source: `totals_by_feature` JSONB | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  feat->>'feature' AS "Feature",
  SUM((feat->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions",
  SUM((feat->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_feature) AS feat
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `totals_by_feature`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Unnests the `totals_by_feature` JSONB array and groups by `feature` name. Shows interaction volume and lines accepted per Copilot surface.

Typical feature values:
- `completions` — inline code completions triggered automatically as the developer types
- `inline_chat` — code suggestions via inline chat (keyboard shortcut in the editor)
- `chat_panel` — interactions in the Copilot Chat sidebar
- `agent_edit` — agent or edit mode direct file writes (not counted in suggestion-based metrics)

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  feat->>'feature' AS "Feature",
  SUM((feat->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions",
  SUM((feat->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_feature) AS feat
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST
```

panels.title: IDE Usage
panels.description: Which IDE(s) this developer uses Copilot in. Source: `totals_by_ide` JSONB | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  ide_row->>'ide' AS "IDE",
  SUM((ide_row->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions",
  SUM((ide_row->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_ide) AS ide_row
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_ide IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `totals_by_ide`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Unnests the `totals_by_ide` JSONB array and groups by `ide` name. Shows interactions and accepted lines per IDE.

Typical IDE values: `vscode`, `jetbrains`, `vim`, `neovim`, `visual_studio`, `xcode`, `azure_data_studio`.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  ide_row->>'ide' AS "IDE",
  SUM((ide_row->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions",
  SUM((ide_row->>'loc_added_sum')::bigint) AS "Lines Accepted"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_ide) AS ide_row
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_ide IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST
```

panels.title: AI Model & Chat Mode Usage - Learning Guide
AI Model Usage (Chat)
panels.description: Which AI models this user's chat interactions use (chat only, not completions). Source: `totals_by_model_feature` JSONB | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  m->>'model' AS "Model",
  SUM((m->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_model_feature) AS m
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_model_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST

panels.title: Chat Mode Breakdown
panels.description: Chat panel interactions by mode (ask, edit, agent, plan). Source: `totals_by_model_feature` JSONB → `feature` field | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  m->>'feature' AS "Chat Mode",
  SUM((m->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions"
FROM copilot_user_daily,
  jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_model_feature) AS m
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_model_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `totals_by_model_feature`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

Unnests the `totals_by_model_feature` JSONB array. Per the API docs, this field covers **chat activity only** (not inline completions). Each element has a `model` and a `feature` (chat mode).

**AI Model Usage (left)** — groups by `model`. When auto-select is on, the actual model used (not "Auto") is attributed.

**Chat Mode Breakdown (right)** — groups by `feature`. Mode values:
- `ask` — question-and-answer in the chat sidebar
- `edit` — Copilot edit mode (modifies the current file)
- `agent` — multi-step agent mode spanning multiple files
- `plan` — planning mode (proposes a plan before coding)
- `custom` — custom agent extensions

### SQL Queries

```sql
-- AI Model Usage
SELECT m->>'model' AS "Model",
  SUM((m->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions"
FROM copilot_user_daily, jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_model_feature) AS m
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_model_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST

-- Chat Mode Breakdown
SELECT m->>'feature' AS "Chat Mode",
  SUM((m->>'user_initiated_interaction_count')::bigint) AS "Interactions"
FROM copilot_user_daily, jsonb_array_elements(totals_by_model_feature) AS m
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_model_feature IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC NULLS LAST
```

panels.title: CLI Sessions (28d)
panels.description: Distinct `gh copilot` CLI sessions. Source: `totals_by_cli` (JSON object, not array) → `session_count` | Table: `copilot_user_daily`

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'session_count')::bigint), 0) AS "CLI Sessions (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'request_count')::bigint), 0) AS "CLI Requests (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'prompt_count')::bigint), 0) AS "CLI Prompts (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL

panels.title: CLI Output Tokens (28d)
panels.description: Total CLI output tokens over the selected time range.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'output_tokens_sum')::bigint), 0) AS "CLI Output Tokens (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL

panels.title: CLI Prompt Tokens (28d)
panels.description: Total CLI prompt tokens over the selected time range.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'prompt_tokens_sum')::bigint), 0) AS "CLI Prompt Tokens (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL

panels.title: CLI Avg Tokens/Request
panels.description: Average total CLI tokens per request over the selected time range.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(
  (COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'output_tokens_sum')::numeric), 0)
  + COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'prompt_tokens_sum')::numeric), 0))
  / NULLIF(COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'request_count')::numeric), 0), 0),
  1
), 0) AS "CLI Avg Tokens/Request"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [→ docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` → `totals_by_cli`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily` — one row per user per day.

### Important: Daily Values, Not Rolling Totals

Despite the report name (`users-28-day`), each NDJSON line returned by the API represents **a single calendar day's activity** for one user — not a 28-day rolling aggregate. The `28-day` in the endpoint name refers to the report's time window (it returns the last 28 days of data), not the aggregation period of each row.

**Evidence:** Values fluctuate significantly day-to-day (e.g. a user may have 138 requests one day and 1,219 the next). If these were rolling 28-day totals, values would be far more stable. Additionally, the table contains exactly one row per user per active day.

Because each row is a **daily snapshot**, the `SUM()` across the selected time range correctly produces the total for that period — there is no double-counting.

### How It's Calculated

`totals_by_cli` is a JSON object on each daily row. These CLI cards sum daily values across the selected time range:

- **CLI Sessions** — `session_count` — distinct `gh copilot` invocations, summed
- **CLI Requests** — `request_count` — total API calls, including automated follow-ups
- **CLI Prompts** — `prompt_count` — user-typed prompts only
- **CLI Output Tokens** — `token_usage.output_tokens_sum` — total generated tokens
- **CLI Prompt Tokens** — `token_usage.prompt_tokens_sum` — total prompt tokens sent
- **CLI Avg Tokens/Request** — `(output_tokens_sum + prompt_tokens_sum) / request_count`

Token counts can be large (hundreds of millions over 28 days for active CLI/agent-mode users) because each request sends full file context as prompt tokens.

Rows where `totals_by_cli IS NULL` (user had no CLI activity that day) are excluded.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'session_count')::bigint), 0) AS "Sessions",
  COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'request_count')::bigint), 0) AS "Requests",
  COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'prompt_count')::bigint), 0) AS "Prompts",
  COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'output_tokens_sum')::bigint), 0) AS "Output Tokens",
  COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'prompt_tokens_sum')::bigint), 0) AS "Prompt Tokens",
  COALESCE(ROUND(
    (COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'output_tokens_sum')::numeric), 0)
    + COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'prompt_tokens_sum')::numeric), 0))
    / NULLIF(COALESCE(SUM((totals_by_cli->>'request_count')::numeric), 0), 0),
    1
  ), 0) AS "Avg Tokens/Request"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL
```

panels.description: How CLI usage metrics are sourced and aggregated for the selected time range.

panels.title: Daily CLI Usage Trend
panels.description: Daily trend of CLI sessions, requests, prompts, and token usage for the selected user. Source: `totals_by_cli` from `copilot_user_daily`.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE((totals_by_cli->>'session_count')::bigint, 0) AS "Sessions"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY day

SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE((totals_by_cli->>'request_count')::bigint, 0) AS "Requests"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY day

SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE((totals_by_cli->>'prompt_count')::bigint, 0) AS "Prompts"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY day

SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'output_tokens_sum')::bigint, 0) AS "Output Tokens"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY day

SELECT
  day AS time,
  COALESCE((totals_by_cli->'token_usage'->>'prompt_tokens_sum')::bigint, 0) AS "Prompt Tokens"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
  AND totals_by_cli IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY day

panels.title: PR Review Time — Learning Guide - Learning Guide
Median PR Review Time (hrs)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1
) AS "Median PR Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

panels.title: P90 PR Review Time (hrs)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT ROUND(
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1
) AS "P90 PR Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

panels.title: PRs Merged

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS "PRs Merged"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source

GitHub REST API — Pull Requests endpoint.

### Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`

### How It's Calculated

**PR Review Time** = `merged_at - created_at` — elapsed time from when a PR was opened to when it was merged.

Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` — **PRs this developer personally merged**, regardless of who wrote the code. Captures both their own PRs and Copilot-agent PRs they reviewed and shipped.

- **Median (P50)** — half of PRs merged faster, half slower. Resistant to outliers.
- **P90** — 90% of PRs merged within this time. Highlights the slow tail.
- **Count** — total PRs merged in the selected window.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT ROUND(
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
    ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
  )::numeric, 1
) AS "Median PR Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
```

panels.title: Weekly PR Review Time Trend — Learning Guide - Learning Guide
Weekly PR Review Time Trend

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  ROUND(
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
      ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
    )::numeric, 1
  ) AS "Median Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source

GitHub REST API — Pull Requests endpoint.

### Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`

### How It's Calculated

Weekly median of `merged_at - created_at` for PRs merged by this user. Bucketed by `date_trunc('week', merged_at)`.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', pr.merged_at) AS time,
  ROUND(
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
      ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0
    )::numeric, 1
  ) AS "Median Review Time (hrs)"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1
```

panels.title: Recent PRs — Learning Guide - Learning Guide
Recent PRs Merged by This User

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  pr.number AS "PR #",
  pr.title AS "Title",
  pr.user->>'login' AS "Author Login",
  CASE
    WHEN pr.user->>'login' LIKE '%[bot]' THEN 'Copilot Bot'
    ELSE 'Human'
  END AS "Author Type",
  ROUND((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0)::numeric, 1) AS "Review Time (hrs)",
  pr.created_at AS "Opened",
  pr.merged_at AS "Merged"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY pr.merged_at DESC
LIMIT 25

panels.description: Source: `pull_requests` table | Filtered by `merged_by_login = user` | Tables: `pull_requests`

options.content:
### GitHub API Source

GitHub REST API — Pull Requests endpoint.

### Database Table(s)

`pull_requests`

### How It's Calculated

25 most recently merged PRs within the selected time range where `merged_by_login = user`. **Author Login** shows the PR opener, and **Author Type** classifies that login as human or bot. Review Time = `merged_at - created_at` in hours, colour-coded green (<24h) / yellow (24-72h) / red (>72h).

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT
  pr.number AS "PR #",
  pr.title AS "Title",
  pr.user_login AS "Author Login",
  CASE
    WHEN pr.user_login LIKE '%[bot]' THEN 'Copilot Bot'
    ELSE 'Human'
  END AS "Author Type",
  ROUND((EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (pr.merged_at - pr.created_at)) / 3600.0)::numeric, 1) AS "Review Time (hrs)",
  pr.created_at AS "Opened",
  pr.merged_at AS "Merged"
FROM pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.merged_by_login = '${user_login}'
  AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
  AND pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
ORDER BY pr.merged_at DESC
LIMIT 25
```

panels.title: Lines Deleted & Net Lines Changed - Learning Guide

options.content:
### GitHub API Source [docs](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/copilot/copilot-usage-metrics?apiVersion=2026-03-10)

`GET /orgs/{org}/copilot/metrics/reports/users-28-day/latest` -> `loc_deleted_sum`, `loc_added_sum`

### Database Table(s)

`copilot_user_daily`

### How It's Calculated

**Lines Deleted (28d)** -> `SUM(loc_deleted_sum)`. Lines removed from the editor by Copilot, primarily through agent mode and edit mode direct file writes. Does not include lines the developer manually deleted.

**Net Lines Changed (28d)** -> `SUM(loc_added_sum) - SUM(loc_deleted_sum)`. Positive = Copilot net-contributed new code. Near-zero or negative = Copilot is doing significant cleanup or refactoring.

Also see the **Daily Activity Trend** chart above. It includes a red dashed series for Lines Deleted.

### SQL Query

```sql
SELECT SUM(loc_deleted_sum) AS "Lines Deleted (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

SELECT SUM(loc_added_sum) - COALESCE(SUM(loc_deleted_sum), 0) AS "Net Lines Changed (28d)"
FROM copilot_user_daily
WHERE user_login = '${user_login}'
  AND day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
```
